items.briefing:
A recent report highlights a growing risk for the education sector: breaches caused by third-party vendors. Registered training organisations often share student and staff data with external software providers, and a compromise at one of those vendors can expose your organisation even if your own systems are secure. This is a timely reminder to check what data your suppliers can access and whether your contracts include security obligations.

Separately, Australia's communications regulator ACMA has announced its 2026–27 compliance priorities, with a focus on protecting consumers from scams and harmful content. Australian organisations should be aware that regulatory scrutiny is increasing.

**Practical tip:** Review your list of third-party software providers and confirm each one has a clear data breach notification process in place.

featured.title: ACMA’s 2026–27 compliance priorities focus on protecting consumers
featured.quote: Australian regulator sets consumer protection priorities, directly affecting local organizations' compliance obligations.
